title: Historical PM Domain Staging Dry-Run Migration
historical_note: This packet records one bounded PM domain dry-run-migration slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `pm-schema-009c` PM-domain dry-run-migration slice
objective: Execute a staging-only dry-run V1-to-V2 PM migration using the validated work schema, the packet-009 mapping rules, the packet-009a mapping infrastructure, and the populated source mappings from packet 009b, then produce reconciliation and quality results without changing production systems.
required_inputs: V1 source export or read-only source connection sufficient for staging migration
constrained_outputs: Staging-only migration DML or migration execution artifacts
A dry-run migration handoff note with row-count reconciliation and quality findings
Explicit list of unmappable or deferred records
No production cutover work and no runtime code changes
validation_steps: Confirm dry-run execution occurs only in the staging database
Confirm migrated entities cover projects, work packages, tasks, and assignments per packet-009 scope
Confirm referential integrity, row-count reconciliation, and enum distribution comparison are explicitly reported
Confirm unmappable records and unresolved lineage gaps are surfaced rather than silently dropped
Confirm no production data movement, cutover work, or runtime adoption changes occur in this packet
sql_permission_note: Packet 009c authorizes staging-only migration DML and validation using the mapping rules and infrastructure from packets 009, 009a, and 009b. It does not authorize production migration or runtime changes.
